title: Flags enumerations zero-value members should be named None
description: An enumeration can be decorated with the FlagsAttribute to indicate that it can be used as a bit field: a set of flags, that can be independently set and reset. For example, the following definition of the day of the week: Monday = 1, // 0b00000001 Tuesday = 2, // 0b00000010 Wednesday = 4, // 0b00000100 Thursday = 8, // 0b00001000 Friday = 16, // 0b00010000 Saturday = 32, // 0b00100000 Sunday = 64 // 0b01000000 allows to define special set of days, such as WeekDays and Weekend using the | operator: None = 0, // 0b00000000 Weekdays = Monday | Tuesday | Wednesday | Thursday | Friday, // 0b00011111 Weekend = Saturday | Sunday, // 0b01100000 All = Weekdays | Weekend // 0b01111111 These can be used to write more expressive conditions, taking advantage of bitwise operators and Enum.HasFlag: var someDays = Days.Wednesday | Days.Weekend; // 0b01100100 someDays.HasFlag(Days.Wednesday); // someDays contains Wednesday var mondayAndWednesday = Days.Monday | Days.Wednesday; someDays.HasFlag(mondayAndWednesday); // someDays contains Monday and Wednesday someDays.HasFlag(Days.Monday) || someDays.HasFlag(Days.Wednesday); // someDays contains Monday or Wednesday someDays & Days.Weekend != Days.None; // someDays overlaps with the weekend someDays & Days.Weekdays == Days.Weekdays; // someDays is only made of weekdays
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
[Flags]
enum Permissions
{
    Empty = 0,    // Noncompliant: zero-value member should be named 'None'
    Read = 1,
    Write = 2,
    Execute = 4
}
compliant:
[Flags]
enum Permissions
{
    None = 0,     // Compliant: zero-value member is named 'None'
    Read = 1,
    Write = 2,
    Execute = 4
}

logic.detection_conditions: Detects enums decorated with [Flags] that have a zero-value member not named 'None'.
logic.reasoning: This logic targets enums with the [Flags] attribute and checks for zero-value members not named 'None'. It excludes common false positive contexts such as logging, tests, formatting, comments, config, and generated code. This ensures only relevant violations are detected.

should_trigger:
[Flags]
enum Permissions
{
    Empty = 0,    // Noncompliant: zero-value member should be named 'None'
    Read = 1,
    Write = 2,
    Execute = 4
}

[Flags]
enum Status
{
    Zero = 0,      // Noncompliant
    Active = 1,
    Inactive = 2
}
should_not_trigger:
[Flags]
enum Permissions
{
    None = 0,     // Compliant: zero-value member is named 'None'
    Read = 1,
    Write = 2,
    Execute = 4
}
